---
title: "Rāg Hamīr"
subtitle: "*Bunda pavana puravāi*"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: article
---

::: {.composer-info}
**Composer:** Hitaparakh &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Tāl:** Cautāl (Drut)
:::

## Lyrics {.unnumbered}

::: {.lyrics-block}

**Sthāyī**

> bunda pavana puravāi garaja garaja barakhata ghana

**Antarā**

> umaḍa ghumaḍa cahudisa ghana ati ghera āvata,  
> madana dahana karata tana, una bina kaise rahata bhavana

:::

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal" collapse="true"}
## Reading the notation

Cautāl is a 12-beat cycle divided as **2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2** (vibhāgs marked **X**, **O**, **2**, **O**, **3**, **4**). Each row below shows **one complete cycle**: the twelve columns correspond to beats 1–12, with sam **X** at beat 1, **O** (khālī) at beat 3, **2** at beat 5, **O** (khālī) at beat 7, **3** at beat 9, and **4** at beat 11. 

The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash **-** indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets $\lb{\cdot}$ denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts $\sps{\cdot}$ indicate the starting grace note of a glide; $\tr{X}$ denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); $\mn{X}$ denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).
:::

## Sthāyī {.unnumbered}

::: {.notation-table .cautal}

| **X** |   | **O** |   | **2** |   | **O** |   | **3** |   | **4** |   |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| P | $\lb{-M}$ | D | $\lb{PP}$ | $\lb{\sps{P}G}$ | M | D | - | D | $\lb{\tr{S}N}$ | $\tr{S}$ | D |
| **bun** | **-** | **da** | **pa** | **va** | **na** | **pu** | **-** | **ra** | **vā** | **-** | **i** |
| $\lb{PM}$ | P | $\lb{\sps{P}G}$ | $\lb{\sps{m}G}$ | $\lb{\sps{G}D}$ | P | $\lb{\sps{P}G}$ | G | $\lb{Gm}$ | R | S | S |
| **ga** | **ra** | **ja** | **ga** | **ra** | **ja** | **ba** | **ra** | **kha** | **ta** | **gha** | **na** |

:::

## Antarā {.unnumbered}

::: {.notation-table .cautal}

| **X** |   | **O** |   | **2** |   | **O** |   | **3** |   | **4** |   |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| $\lb{PM}$ | D | P | $\lb{\sps{P}\tr{S}}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | - | $\lb{\tr{S}N}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ |
| **u** | **ma** | **ḍa** | **ghu** | **ma** | **ḍa** | **ca** | **hu** | **-** | **di** | **-** | **sa** |
| $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\lb{\tr{S}D}$ | $\lb{ND}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\lb{\tr{S}N}$ | $\tr{R}$ | $\lb{\tr{S}N}$ | $\tr{S}$ | D | P |
| **gha** | **na** | **-** | **a** | **-** | **ti** | **ghe** | **-** | **ra** | **ā** | **va** | **ta** |
| $\lb{PM}$ | D | P | $\lb{\sps{P}\tr{S}}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | - | $\lb{\tr{S}N}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ |
| **u** | **ma** | **ḍa** | **ghu** | **ma** | **ḍa** | **ca** | **hu** | **-** | **di** | **-** | **sa** |
| $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\lb{\tr{S}D}$ | $\lb{ND}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\lb{\tr{S}N}$ | $\tr{R}$ | $\lb{\tr{S}N}$ | $\tr{S}$ | D | P |
| **gha** | **na** | **-** | **a** | **-** | **ti** | **ghe** | **-** | **ra** | **ā** | **va** | **ta** |
| $\lb{mG}$ | G | $\lb{PM}$ | D | M | P | G | m | R | $\mn{N}$ | R | S |
| **ma** | **da** | **na** | **da** | **ha** | **na** | **ka** | **ra** | **ta** | **-** | **ta** | **na** |
| S | $\tr{R}$ | $\tr{R}$ | $\tr{R}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\lb{\tr{S}N}$ | $\tr{S}$ | D | $\lb{PM}$ | G | m |
| **u** | **na** | **bi** | **na** | **kai** | **se** | **ra** | **ha** | **ta** | **bha** | **va** | **na** |

:::
